overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains an active presence across Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, focusing on research related to social media analysis, disinformation, and computational social science. His content aims to share research findings, announce opportunities (jobs, grants, conference presentations), and engage in discussions about methodological approaches in the field. Total follower count across all platforms is 3580.

contentThemes.description: Sharing research findings, papers, and methodologies related to computational social science.
Content focused on detecting and analyzing disinformation campaigns and coordinated inauthentic behavior.
Announcements of job openings, research grants, and PhD positions in related fields.

recommendations: Increase cross-promotion of content across platforms to leverage audience overlap.
Experiment with different content formats (e.g., videos, infographics) to boost engagement.
Actively participate in relevant conversations and communities to expand reach and influence.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky is used for sharing research publications and conference presentations, along with PhD opportunity announcements. Reposts are a prominent engagement metric.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon focuses on sharing updates on research tools and participating in broader discussions within the academic community. Engagement is lower compared to other platforms.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn is primarily used for announcing research grants and job opportunities, resulting in higher engagement through likes and comments. The professional audience likely contributes to this increased engagement.

topPerformingContent.content: Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.

Now hiring: Two postdoctoral researchers to join our team working on computational methods for social media analysis. Background in data science, computational social science, or related fields preferred.
Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
